findings.note: AC-002/TEST-002 negative controls assert only the first diagnostic rule ID (stage-provenance) and nonzero exit, not the 'task plan hash is stale' detail string named in the AC. The single-variable fixture design makes the stale-hash detail the only reachable stage-provenance failure, so residual risk is negligible, but asserting the detail text would pin the AC exactly.

The implemented CRLF corpus repeats only TEST-001's base-add variant (plus TEST-002(a) and both final-line-no-newline fixtures); the value-edit and multi-occurrence TEST-001 variants are not repeated under CRLF, and the fixture registry is written LF by jq rather than CRLF. Residual risk is very low (deletion is a per-line rule), so this is coverage narrowing rather than a behavioral gap.
The new Second Approval deletion Replace is indented one level deeper than the three sibling masks — cosmetic only; logic and ordering are correct.
